summary: Set 045 Session 4 of 6 — Claude per-event parser + narration v1.1 template + extension command. Ships the symmetric Claude-side counterpart to S3's Copilot per-event parser plus the operator-facing narration tooling: (1) ai_router/narration.py — MARKER_REGEX (Set 044 narration-design.md §2.3 verbatim incl. ASCII + 4 Unicode curly-quote variants), detect_marker() with full §5.5 semantic-check enumeration (placeholder-leakage / unknown-phase / unknown-effort-enum / session-exceeds-total / non-integer-{session,total} / version skipped / incomplete), render_template(kind, set_slug, session_number, total_sessions, effort) that refuses placeholder strings, project_state_for_template() reading via the D13-compliant ai_router.progress.read_progress reader, and a python -m ai_router.narration CLI (--kind {claude,agents}, --state-file XOR --set-slug+--session+--total, --effort, --output). (2) ai_router/joiner/parsers.py read_claude_session_events() — per-event HarvestRecord emission for ~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<conv>.jsonl: first user/assistant record → session_start (once), each assistant record → turn, tool_use blocks → tool_call (redacted via _summarize_claude_tool_args — file paths + line counts + Bash command_head only, no argv body / old_string / new_string leakage), assistant.message.usage → usage (tokens_in summed across input + cache_creation + cache_read; tokens_out from output_tokens), text blocks matching MARKER_REGEX → marker event with source='narration'. Noise types (queue-operation, ai-title, last-prompt, file-history-snapshot, attachment) skipped. Sticky context (cwd, conv_id, model) propagates; provider defaults to 'anthropic' on first model sighting. Tolerant streaming. (3) ai_router/joiner/schema.py _native_events_for() Claude branch dispatches to read_claude_session_events (replaces S3 single session_start fallback); Copilot branch unchanged; future-engines fallback retained. (4) tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/commands/regenerateNarrationTemplates.ts — VS Code command 'Dabbler: Regenerate Narration Templates' picks in-progress session set (auto-select on 1; quickpick otherwise), shells out to python -m ai_router.narration twice inside vscode.window.withProgress, writes <set-dir>/narration-templates/{CLAUDE.md,AGENTS.md}, surfaces a toast with 'Open Rendered CLAUDE.md' + 'Copy to consumer workspace…' actions (quickpick + showOpenDialog folder picker + fs.copyFileSync + overwrite confirm). Wired into extension.ts safeRegister + declared in package.json contributes.commands. (5) docs/narration-templates.md — operator-facing reference covering when to use templates, how to regenerate (extension command + CLI), marker anatomy table, four Q3 defensive rules with ablation-analysis cross-refs, malformed-marker diagnostic-flag semantics. Tests: 18 new Layer-1 (test_narration.py — TestMarkerRegex 3 + TestDetectMarker 9 + TestRenderTemplate 6 incl. defensive-rule prose audit + round-trip + TestProjectStateForTemplate 4 + CLI smoke 2; test_joiner_parsers.py +5 Claude per-event scenarios + 1 Bash-argv redaction). Test fixture update: _write_claude_jsonl in test_dabbler_launch_join_e2e.py now writes a proper type=user record (the S3 minimal no-type fixture survived the old fallback but the new strict per-event parser correctly treats it as noise). Watcher-inventory allowlist updated for the extension.ts line shift from the new safeRegister entry (146 → 147). pytest 808 passed (+33 vs S3) + 1 skipped, no regressions; TS clean (npx tsc --noEmit); extension unit harness 531 passing (2 pre-existing failures unrelated to S4: configEditor-foundation ViewColumn.One stub gap; notificationsSection Set 026 S7 disabled-button assertion). Step 6 verification VERIFIED Round A (gemini-pro session-verification, $0.063, no must-fix); two nice-to-haves applied in-flight (progress wrapper + copy-to-workspace action). Set 045 cumulative routed: $0.024 (S1) + $0.053066 (S2) + $0.107 (S3) + $0.063 (S4) = $0.247 of $5 NTE. Carry-forward: S5 (Explorer integration) inherits working Claude per-event parser w/ marker detection + render-from-state CLI Explorer can shell out to. Two F-section follow-ups worth surfacing pre-S5: Copilot-side marker scanning of gen_ai.output.messages (separate from this set's Claude scope; needs OTel content-capture env var), and Q3 optional A1-A8 ablation pre-S6 Marketplace release (~$1-3) for isolated-trigger defensive posture.

reason.specifics: Session 5 (Explorer integration + Layer-3 coverage) is the largest UI surface change in Set 045. The Session Set Explorer rows gain harvested-signal badges (wrapper-launched, narration-present, native-log-bound from joiner-spec.md §6 CoverageSummary) plus a conflict-warning surface when scan_conflicts() returns engine-mismatch / bare-touch / stale-checkout-touch / writer-bypass reports. Layer-3 Playwright coverage for the new rendering. The TypeScript Explorer code (tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts) reads joiner output via python -m ai_router.joiner CLI; Opus's careful-reasoning + retained Set 045 context (S1 spike findings + S2 spec lock + S3 wrapper output + S4 Claude parser + marker detection) outweighs a Codex/Gemini handoff that would need to re-load the full chain. Per memory project_029_s6_html_preview_iteration, operator wants ~11-iteration HTML-preview loop for visual surface refinement; that loop is best handled in-process with joiner output flowing live. This recommendation is SELF-AUTHORED per the operator's standing in-session router restriction. Two carry-forward operator-decision items worth flagging at S5 start (before code lands): (a) should the Explorer also surface a 'narration marker support' badge per backend (Claude active in S4; Copilot OTel content-capture is a follow-on; Codex/Gemini are future) — this informs whether the empty-state UX shows the operator how to enable the wrapper / narration template; (b) the Q3 optional A1-A8 ablation has not been run — if operator wants 'isolated-trigger defensive posture' before Marketplace release in S6, S5 is a good time to schedule it (~$1-3 of NTE; bounded operator-attention).
